DISCOVERY 610, APPEARANCES: PATRICK A. CONNOLLY FOR DEFENDANT AND MOVING PARTY: BACH CONSTRUCTION INC., PETER BACH AND HANS BACH NO OTHER APPEARANCES STATED. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED WITH PROTECTIVE ORDER: DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS IS DENIED. THE COURT FINDS THAT DEFENDANT DID NOT WAIVE HIS OBJECTION TO THE DEPOSITION OFFICER. CCP SECTION 2020(D)(3) AND EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 1560(E) EXPRESSLY PROVIDE THAT AN ATTORNEY FOR A PARTY MAY SERVE AS THE DEPOSITION OFFICER. DEFENDANT HAS NOT PROVED THAT COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS FINANCIALLY INTERESTED IN THE ACTION. THE SUBPOENA IS MODIFIED TO COMPLY WITH EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 1560(E) TO PROVIDE THAT THE WITNESS MAKE THE RECORDS AVAILABLE FOR INSPECTION OR COPYING BY COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF (BOX "1 C" - JUDICIAL COUNCIL OF CALIFORNIA: DEPOSITION SUBPOENA FOR PRODUCTION OF BUSINESS RECORDS). COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHALL COMPLY WITH THE PROVISIONS OF EVIDENCE CODE SECTIONS 1560(E) AND 1561. COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF IS FURTHER ORDERED TO NOTIFY DEFENDANTS AND ALLOW THEM TO PURCHASE COPIES. THE COURT WILL NOT CONSIDER THE OBJECTIONS TO THE CONTENT OF THE SUBPOENAS RAISED FOR THE FIRST TIME IN DEFENDANT'S REPLY TO THE OPPOSITION TO THE INSTANT MOTION. DEFENDANT HAS FAILED TO COMPLY WITH CALIFORNIA RULE OF COURT 335. THE REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
